Toss Update:  won the toss and elected to field first! 

SPOTLIGHT PERFORMERS

Ayush Mhatre has burst onto the scene as the fearless U19 sensation who plays with intent from the very first ball. In just 7 matches, he’s smashed 240 runs at a jaw dropping 188.98 strike rate, including a scintillating 94 that showed he can dismantle any attack. His aggressive, top order tempo makes him the quintessential X factor for his side — the kind of explosive opener who can set the tone, seize momentum instantly, and force bowlers into panic mode. He’s young, bold, and built for highlight reel moments.

Prabhsimran Singh, meanwhile, is the seasoned power package for Punjab — a right handed batter who’s stitched together three consistent seasons of impactful cricket. With 549 runs in 17 matches at a blazing 160.53 strike rate and a best of 91, he brings reliability wrapped in six hitting fury. When he gets going, the ball disappears into the stands with effortless timing. Prabhsimran is Punjab’s proven game breaker, the X factor who can shift the rhythm of a match and unleash scoreboard pressure in a heartbeat.

 

 

BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 

The matchup between Shivam Dube and Arshdeep Singh is a fascinating left hand bat versus left arm pace battle. Arshdeep has been effective against Dube, conceding just 21 runs off 30 balls while dismissing him three times, showing his ability to cramp Dube for room with swing and smart variations. While Dube is known for his power hitting, Arshdeep’s discipline and deft use of angles have often kept the CSK all rounder in check during their encounters.

The contest between Sanju Samson and Yuzvendra Chahal highlights a classic duel of skill and patience. The right handed Samson has scored 49 runs from 59 balls, but Chahal has had the upper hand with five dismissals, underlining the leg spinner’s knack for outthinking aggressive batters. Samson enjoys taking on spinners, yet Chahal’s flight, guile, and ability to induce false shots have repeatedly tilted the balance in his favor, making this a key tactical matchup whenever they face off.


 


HEAD TO HEAD IN IPL 

Since the 2023 IPL season, the head to head battle between Chennai Super Kings (CSK) and Punjab Kings has clearly tilted in Punjab’s favor. Across 5 matches, Punjab Kings have won 4, while CSK have managed just 1 victory, showing Punjab’s recent dominance in this rivalry. Despite CSK’s rich legacy and experience, Punjab have consistently had the upper hand in recent encounters, often outplaying CSK in key moments and making this matchup increasingly competitive and intriguing for fans.




VENUE STATS:

 

MATCH PREVIEW 
Chepauk gets ready to welcome their favourite men in yellow as Chennai Super Kings lock horns with Punjab Kings under the Madras lights. This venue demands craft, control, and serious cricketing IQ where slow burn turns into late over chaos. CSK coming off a loss against Rajasthan Royals in game 01 will look to get back to winning ways at home while Shreyas Iyers men are coming off a comprehensive win against Gujarat Titans . This game has all the pedigree for a blockbuster as both teams pack a punch with their opening combinations . 

CSK will want Ruturaj Gaikwad to come good against the swing of Arshdeep , while Sanju the new fan favourite will look to set the tone with his clean hitting at the start . Punjab Kings will back their pace quadruple of Arshdeep , Bartlett , Jansen & Vyshak as all four can take the pace off and hit the deck hard providing vital breakthroughs at crucial junctures of the game . CSK 's middle order barring Jamie Overton will have to find their feet against the spin of IPL veteran Yuzvendra Chahal . As for Punjab Kings they will look to decimate the home team to continue their winning streak with their young opening combination of Prabhsimran Singh & Priyansh Arya and not to forget the debut dasher Cooper Conolly in the middle order . Anshul Kamboj & Khaleel Ahmed have the ability to generate lateral movement under the lights & Anbu den will be on the edge of their seats wanting to see a tight contest with bat & ball 

Throwback to MS Dhoni's heroics in 2010 , Maxwell's fireworks in 2014 , KL Rahul's sublime hundred in 2020 to last over humdinger in 2023 these two teams have played some intense games over the years in the toughest twenty . Come the 3rd of April Men in Yellow & Red will look to repeat the same brand of bold cricket and fans of the yellow brigade brace yourselves for an  exuberant game of cricket to kick of TATA IPL fever in Chennai.
MatchSummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Priyansh Arya (Man of the Match) :
“It’s something that’s been part of my game right from the start. I’ve always tried to bat in a way that benefits the team at the end of the day, rather than overthinking the situation.”
“There’s obviously an element of risk involved, but I have a strong belief in my ability. If I feel the first ball is there to be hit, I back myself to go for it.”
“There’s definitely a step up from the DPL to the IPL. At this level, you’re facing far more experienced bowlers, which tests you as a batter and pushes you to improve.”


Shreyas Iyer (Punjab Kings Captain) :
“There’s always a bit of concern before going out to chase a big total, but the way we began today was outstanding. Priyansh and Prabh did a terrific job up front, giving us the perfect platform going into the middle overs. They’ve been batting this way for a long time now, and that consistency really helps settle the rhythm for the rest of us.”
“I’m really pleased that everyone has had the chance to bat within the first couple of games. It builds confidence across the group. When we find ourselves under pressure, people know exactly how to handle the situation and play their roles.”
“There wasn’t anything planned as such. We simply spoke in the dressing room about wanting to impress each other rather than focusing on outside expectations. That mindset has worked well for us.”

Ruturaj Gaikwad (Chennai Super Kings Captain) :
“I honestly felt it was a very good total. The pitch offered plenty for the fast bowlers, while the spinners were getting a bit of grip. Considering the big boundaries at this ground, I thought we had posted a strong score.”
“We already had three seamers in the side, and the idea was to rely on our wrist spinners after the power play. Unfortunately, both of them had a slightly off day, but that was the thinking behind the decision.”
“It was a mix of both execution and timing. We bowled well in patches but failed to do it at the crucial moments. The pressure never truly built. The run rate hovered around 11 — two solid overs could have pushed it to 12 or 13, but we couldn’t maintain sustained pressure.”
“It’s definitely a tough loss, but there are plenty of positives to take. Ayush played an outstanding innings and gave us a lot of confidence going forward. Shivam was impressive as well. As a unit, our bowling was slightly off tonight.”
“I’m feeling good personally. It’s about adapting to what the team requires at any given time. With the pitch gripping a bit and Ayush batting fluently at the other end, I decided to play a supporting role. Unfortunately, I got out at the wrong moment.”

Punjab Kings produced a confident and well paced chase to defeat Chennai Super Kings by five wickets in Match 7 at the MA Chidambaram Stadium, Chennai, on 3 April 2026, successfully overhauling 209 with 8 balls to spare.

After being put in to bat, CSK posted a formidable 209/5 in their 20 overs, built around a dominant middle order performance. Young batter Ayush Mhatre was the standout, hammering 73 off 43 balls, striking six fours and five sixes to anchor the innings after early setbacks. CSK lost Sanju Samson and skipper Ruturaj Gaikwad cheaply, but Mhatre’s 96 run partnership with Gaikwad steadied the innings and laid the platform.

The late surge was powered by Shivam Dube’s unbeaten 45 off 27 balls, complemented by a blistering cameo from Sarfaraz Khan, whose 32 off just 12 deliveries injected crucial momentum in the death overs. CSK added 81 runs in the final five overs, capitalizing on wayward bowling, as Punjab conceded 15 wides overall.

In reply, Punjab Kings paced their chase with composure, keeping the required rate under control despite the pressure of a big total. The batting unit showed excellent intent through the middle and death overs, finding regular boundaries and punishing loose deliveries. Their ability to close out the chase in 18.4 overs underlined a clinical batting performance on a good surface.

For CSK, the bowling struggled to apply consistent pressure, with none of the bowlers managing to stem the flow decisively at the back end. Punjab’s calm approach in crunch moments proved decisive, as they sealed a valuable away victory and important early season momentum.
